content: Rewriting git history (filter-repo/BFG) only affects the origin repo going forward -- new clones/fetches won't see the removed file. It does NOT retroactively remove the data from: existing local clones, existing forks, CI caches, GitHub's own cached views/PR diffs of old commits, or any copy already made before the rewrite. There is no way to force-delete data from clones/forks you don't control; the only real mitigation is to never have committed the protected content in the first place, or treat any past-committed secret/protected file as permanently compromised once pushed to a public remote.
